assets.name: lecture_07_figure_02.png
assets.rationale: This frame gives the clearest and most settled view of the board in this subtitle window. The displayed Lagrangian is fully visible, centered, and unobstructed, and the operator expression above it is still legible enough to preserve the local board context. The earlier candidates are essentially the same setup, so the latest frame is the best documentary capture.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: of motion, the Lagrangian equations of motion, straightforward.

assets.name: lecture_07_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: This is the clearest and least obstructed frame in the window. The notebook and hand that block the earlier candidates are gone, leaving the board equation fully visible and centered. It is the most useful documentary frame for notes because the key relation can be read directly without occlusion.

assets.name: lecture_07_figure_05.png
assets.rationale: This frame is the cleanest wide shot of the board in the subtitle window. All three written lines are visible at once, and the lecturer has not yet moved far enough into frame to block the left side of the equations. It is the best documentary image for the moment where Susskind explains building the super Lagrangian from powers of the superfield and its derivatives.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: our super Lagrangian out of powers of phi, derivatives of phi, and so forth.
